stories.title: Son vs. Luna: LAFC host Real Salt Lake in midweek showdown
stories.summary: LAFC hosts Real Salt Lake at BMO Stadium on Wednesday evening. The match is a midweek showdown between Western Conference contenders, with LAFC currently standing third in the standings with 27 points. Son Heung-Min and Denis Bouanga are key players to watch, having scored in LAFC's last game against LA Galaxy. The match will be broadcast on Apple TV, FS1, and FOX Deportes.

stories.title: 2026 Kia WNBA Shooting Stars Participants
stories.summary: Four WNBA legends, including two-time MVP Elena Delle Donne and three-time MVP Lauren Jackson, will compete in the Kia WNBA Shooting Stars timed shooting competition on July 24 at 8 p.m. ET, live on ESPN from Wintrust Arena in Chicago. The event is part of the AT&T WNBA All-Star 2026, which will be followed by the 2026 AT&T WNBA All-Star Game on July 25. The competition features four teams representing the WNBA's Minnesota, Seattle, Washington, and Dallas/Detroit markets. The games will be broadcast on multiple platforms.

stories.title: Jordan accuses Smith of lying to Congress in criminal referral to DOJ
stories.summary: House Judiciary Chair Jim Jordan has referred former special counsel Jack Smith to the Justice Department for potential criminal prosecution, accusing Smith of lying to Congress about his investigation into President Trump. Jordan claims Smith made false statements about obtaining lawmakers' text messages, which were actually obtained through the White House. The referral was made in a letter to the acting Attorney General on Wednesday. The Justice Department will now review the allegations.

stories.title: Cubs, Tigers face off in rubber match (LIVE on ESPN)
stories.summary: The Chicago Cubs face the Detroit Tigers in the rubber match of their series, with the Cubs looking to take the win after a 11-2 victory in the second game. The matchup is exclusively televised on ESPN at 8:10 p.m. ET. The Cubs have had a rollercoaster season, with two 10-game winning streaks and a 10-game losing streak. The team's success is largely due to the development of Pete Crow-Armstrong, who has 5.9 WAR and a .905 OPS.

stories.title: Keider Montero unleashing inner bulldog in bullpen role with Tigers
stories.summary: Keider Montero is returning to the starting rotation for the Detroit Tigers, starting against the Chicago Cubs at Wrigley Field on July 22. Montero had previously excelled in a bullpen role, making 13 starts and five quality starts earlier in the season. He will join a temporary six-man rotation, with the team looking to give pitchers more rest time during a 13-game stretch. This move allows Montero to pitch every fifth day, a schedule he has grown accustomed to.

stories.title: Hosepipe ban in force for millions of households - is your area affected?
stories.summary: A hosepipe ban has taken effect for over 20 million households in southern and eastern England and parts of Wales, restricting use for watering gardens, washing vehicles and windows, and filling pools. The ban is in place due to heatwaves straining water supplies. This measure affects a significant portion of the UK population. As the country faces drier summers with climate change, concerns are raised about water resource management.

stories.title: Lego’s Donkey Kong arcade machine lets Mario jump endless barrels — Miyamoto is reportedly happy
stories.summary: A Lego Donkey Kong arcade machine has been designed, priced at $200, featuring a working joystick and button to control Mario's movement and jumping. The game includes an endless series of barrels for Mario to jump, utilizing the same original barrels first released in 1989 for Lego's pirate sets. The machine is said to have won the favor of Mario creator Shigeru Miyamoto. The set is now available, offering a unique nostalgic gaming experience.

stories.title: China's Moonshot AI stole from Anthropic, Trump tech adviser says
stories.summary: A White House adviser has accused China's Moonshot AI of stealing capabilities from top US AI models, specifically Anthropic's Fable AI, through a process called distillation. The US government claims Moonshot AI accessed restricted Nvidia servers to train its K3 model, which was unveiled last week. The K3 model is said to rival top US technology. The US government is now investigating the allegations.

stories.title: Even camels can't cope: Africa's ships of the desert hit by rising temperatures
stories.summary: Rising temperatures in Africa's Afar region are causing extreme heat stress in camels, with herder Ali Umer reporting eight camel calves lost in the past month due to blistering heat, watery eyes, and skin burns. The region is one of the hottest and driest places on Earth, where camels are used to carry salt through the desert. Temperatures are becoming increasingly intense, with even the wind now carrying heat. Herders are struggling to cope with the changing climate.

stories.title: Where to Find a Commonwealth Games 2026 Livestream in the U.S. to Watch the Event for Free
stories.summary: The 2026 Commonwealth Games will take place in Glasgow, Scotland, from July 23 to August 2, featuring over 7,000 athletes from more than 70 nations. The event will be livestreamed for free on CBC in Canada, accessible to North American viewers via a virtual private network like ExpressVPN. Viewers can set their location to Canada to stream the coverage online. The Games will air live and as nightly highlights on channels like 7Plus in Australia and Channel 5 in the UK.

stories.title: U.S.-Saudi Nuclear Deal Stokes Israeli Concerns of Mideast Arms Race
stories.summary: An Israeli minister sought to downplay the agreement, while critics said it could spur other regional countries to pursue a nuclear program, and potentially the creation of a bomb.

stories.title: Amish Shah Wins Democratic Primary in Swing Arizona House District
stories.summary: Dr. Amish Shah, who beat the choice of the party’s House campaign arm, will face Jay Feely, a Republican, in a battleground district that covers parts of Phoenix.

stories.title: Republican Lawmaker Urges Charges Against Jack Smith
stories.summary: The criminal referral could provide a catalyst for a prosecution of Mr. Smith, who as special counsel became a top target of the president’s retribution campaign.

stories.title: US signs landmark nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia
stories.summary: The US and Saudi Arabia have signed a landmark nuclear deal, a "peaceful nuclear co-operation agreement," allowing Saudi Arabia to develop a civilian nuclear program. The deal includes a bilateral safeguards agreement and could provide great access for American companies. The US Department of Energy estimates the partnership could be worth billions of dollars over decades. The agreement's text remains undisclosed, but experts warn it may increase the risk of nuclear proliferation in the region.

stories.title: Canada's 'powerful' dairy sector is in Trump's trade crosshairs
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump has announced a 50% tariff on $20 billion worth of Canadian goods, citing Canada's dairy supply management system as one of the main irritants. The system, which includes production quotas, set pricing, and import quotas, is seen as "unreasonable" by American farmers who want to sell their product in Canada. The tariff is set to take effect in August. Canadian politicians, including Quebec Premier Christine Fréchette, have indicated that dairy supply management is non-negotiable.

stories.title: Three Airports Plan to Ditch T.S.A. Agents Amid Push for Private Security
stories.summary: The airports have opted in to the agency’s new security model, an agency spokesperson confirmed on Wednesday. A union official said the move could jeopardize public safety.

stories.title: Most sewage spills in England may be illegal, new research suggests
stories.summary: New research suggests that up to 86% of raw sewage spills in England may be illegal, with most occurring during non-heavy rain conditions. The analysis, covering over 350 storm overflow outlets, found that water companies serving more than 30 million people failed to meet regulations. This raises concerns about the effectiveness of current sewage management practices. The findings may lead to increased scrutiny of England's water companies and potential enforcement action.

stories.title: Houthis Claim Strikes on 2 Saudi Oil Tankers in Red Sea
stories.summary: The Saudi authorities had yet to confirm the hits, which would be the first attacks since the Iranian-backed Yemeni group announced a blockade on Saudi Arabia.

stories.title: Hope for thousands of MS patients as 'life-changing' drug now on NHS in England
stories.summary: A life-changing treatment for thousands of multiple sclerosis patients in England is now available on the NHS. Up to 5,000 people could benefit each year from the drug fampridine, which boosts nerve signals to help people walk and live independently. Clinical trials showed around four in 10 people taking the drug had improvements in their walking speed and endurance. This development is expected to significantly improve the quality of life for those affected by the debilitating disease.

stories.title: Airport drop-off fees up by a third - here are the priciest
stories.summary: Drop-off fees at the UK's major airports have increased by a third on average since last summer, with two London airports, Gatwick and Stansted, charging the highest fees at £10. The fees are part of airports' business models and climate change objectives. London City Airport now charges the highest per-minute rate at £1.60 per minute. The UK's airports are adopting these fees as they aim to reduce car usage and encourage passengers to use trains and buses.

stories.title: McInnes glad of Rangers backing in appeal against ban
stories.summary: Rangers manager Derek McInnes has received a four-match touchline ban for criticizing a late penalty awarded to Celtic at Motherwell in May. The comments were made when McInnes was in charge of Hearts, and Rangers are now appealing the Scottish FA's decision. McInnes has stated he disagrees with the ban and is glad the club is contesting it. The appeal will likely be a key focus for the club in the coming weeks.

stories.title: House Passes Defense Bill Amid Iran War Divide
stories.summary: Republicans pushed through the $1.15 trillion bill over opposition from Democrats, who said backing it was akin to embracing the war.

stories.title: Google burning through cash with spiralling AI costs
stories.summary: Google's parent company Alphabet reported negative free cash flow of $5.9 billion for the first time in at least a decade, due to soaring spending on artificial intelligence infrastructure. The company's AI costs are expected to reach $205 billion this year, up from $190 billion. This significant increase in spending comes as major tech companies invest heavily in AI technology. As a result, Alphabet's stock fell 4% in after-hours trading.

stories.title: Yeon Sang-ho Returns to ‘Parasyte’ Universe With ‘Tamiya,’ Starring Shioli Kutsuna, at Netflix
stories.summary: South Korean filmmaker Yeon Sang-ho is returning to the "Parasyte" universe with a new live-action series, "Parasyte: Tamiya," starring Shioli Kutsuna, set to stream on Netflix in 2027. The series marks a collaboration between Netflix and Yeon, who co-wrote the script with Ryu Yong-jae. This is Yeon's third project with Netflix, following "Hellbound" and "Parasyte: The Grey." The series is expected to premiere next year.

stories.title: Clarity Act Mired In Debate Over Whether to Bar President From Selling Crypto
stories.summary: Democrats and Republicans are haggling about the Clarity Act, a major bill pushed by the crypto industry, as it moves closer to a Senate vote.

stories.title: Shaken, Soaked and Seasick: What It’s Like to Survive ‘The Odyssey’ in 4DX
stories.summary: A 3-hour film, "The Odyssey," starring Matt Damon, was screened in 4DX format at select theaters. The immersive experience features seat-rumbling and water-spritzing technology, simulating the epic adventure on screen. Over 100 theaters worldwide have adopted this format, offering a unique cinematic experience. Audiences are left shaken, soaked, and seasick, a testament to the film's immersive storytelling.

stories.title: Tears and sleepless nights as Marshall and Adlington get Scotland flag honour
stories.summary: Scottish athletes Marshall and Adlington have been chosen to carry the Scotland flag at the Commonwealth Games. Marshall, a 59-year-old bowls player, and Adlington, a 39-year-old judo athlete, will share the honour. The flag will be returned to its owners after the ceremony, but the two athletes may have to fight for the first photo opportunity. The event is set to take place at the Commonwealth Games, where Adlington is also competing to complete a treble.

stories.title: Liverpool confident of signing Barcola - Thursday's gossip
stories.summary: Liverpool is confident of signing Paris St-Germain forward Bradley Barcola, a 23-year-old who impressed with four goal contributions at the World Cup with France this summer. The move would come after beating Arsenal and Bayern Munich to the signature. Talks are ongoing, but no official deal has been announced. Liverpool's pursuit of Barcola is a significant development in the transfer market.

stories.title: Target ultra-processed food to reduce obesity in Britain | Letter
stories.summary: A British health select committee report has found that obesity is an engineered outcome, not a failure of willpower. The report targets ultra-processed foods, which are high in sugar and refined carbohydrates, as primary drivers of weight gain and type 2 diabetes. The committee recommends a new framework to hold supermarkets and manufacturers accountable. The UK government is expected to review food policy in response to the report's findings.

stories.title: What Were the Lotus-Eaters Eating in Homer’s ‘Odyssey’?
stories.summary: In Homer’s epic, Odysseus’s men eat something so powerful that they forget about home. Which plants could have produced such a soporific, narcotic and amnesiac effect?
